target_test_run.notes: Live tests (test_live_embeddings_are_real_nonzero_vectors, test_retrieve_documents_reasonable_recall_on_fixture_queries, test_top_hit_for_unambiguous_query_is_the_correct_document, test_select_chunk_config_grid_search_matches_shipped_defaults) ran against a real local Ollama server (ollama pull nomic-embed-text executed in this session, 274MB, model confirmed present via `ollama list`) -- NOT mocked. test_live_embeddings_are_real_nonzero_vectors explicitly asserts two different sentences produce different, non-all-zero vectors, directly confirming real embeddings (not a stub) were used.

whole_repo_regression_run.notes: Confirms zero regressions to any pre-existing test (including the real live-Ollama+real-engine test_e2e_smoke.py::test_full_pipeline_smoke, which also passed as part of this full run). This is a pure-addition subtask -- 386 = pre-existing test count + this subtask's 12 new tests (0 modified/deleted tests).

chunk_tuning_sweep.method: select_chunk_config(_FIXTURE_DOCS, _FIXTURE_QUERIES, real OllamaEmbeddingClient(), top_k=3), run standalone against the real local Ollama server before finalizing DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE_WORDS/DEFAULT_OVERLAP_WORDS
chunk_tuning_sweep.grid: chunk_size in {80, 150, 220} words x overlap_fraction in {0.0, 0.15, 0.30}
chunk_tuning_sweep.tie_break_rule: Among configs tied at the maximum mean recall, prefer the smaller chunk size, then the smaller overlap (leaner index, no accuracy cost observed at this fixture scale).
chunk_tuning_sweep.disclosed_limitation: Most candidates tied at perfect recall@3 on this small (5-doc, 5-query) fixture corpus; only (220, 33) showed a measurable recall drop (0.9), consistent with architecture-discovery.md's disclosure that a genuinely discriminating tuning sweep needs a larger/harder corpus than a fixture can practically provide. The fixture corpus was deliberately built as multi-section 'handbook' documents (not one-topic-per-doc) specifically to create some real discrimination rather than a trivial universal tie, and this is confirmed to have worked (the one non-1.0 result). A future real-corpus-scale benchmark subtask (5.3.4-style) is expected to re-run this same select_chunk_config sweep against the real combined corpus, where discrimination between candidates is expected to be much sharper; this subtask's chosen defaults are an honest, reproducible, non-arbitrary starting point given the explicitly fixture-scoped self-test, not a claim that (80,0) is the globally optimal config at production scale.

constraint_compliance_recheck.no_new_pip_dependency: confirmed -- agents/pyproject.toml untouched (git diff shows no changes to it)
constraint_compliance_recheck.no_live_large_scale_benchmark_execution: confirmed -- grep for 'datasets'/'synthetic_corpus' across the 2 new eval/ files returns matches only inside docstring/comment prose (disclosing the scope boundary); neither new file contains an `import` statement referencing agents.eval.datasets or a file-read of data/synthetic_corpus/
